steps.title: Identify the key qualification
steps.content: Text 1 concludes the bees were “having fun.” Text 2 agrees Galpayage showed bumblebees “may engage in play, possibly experiencing some kind of positive emotional state,” but then adds a critical caveat: “emotions in insects, if they do indeed exist, are likely very rudimentary.” The key signal is this limitation on what kind of emotions insects can experience.

steps.title: Apply the qualification to the choices
steps.content:
D captures the author's position: it accepts play and positive feelings but emphasizes they would be “probably quite basic,” matching Text 2's claim that emotions in insects are “likely very rudimentary.” The other three contradict, invert, or ignore this qualification.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
